description: You are a GEO stationkeeping analyst at a commercial satellite operator. You need to characterize the natural solar radiation pressure (SRP) perturbation on the SATCOM-9 satellite's eccentricity vector to design a 'sun-pointing perigee' stationkeeping strategy. Using the specifications in ~/Desktop/geo_srp_spec.txt, create and run a GMAT script (~/GMAT_output/geo_srp_mission.script) that propagates the spacecraft for 365 days with a 1-day step size. The force model must include SRP (with specified Cr and area) and Sun/Moon point masses, without atmospheric drag. Generate an orbital element report at ~/GMAT_output/geo_srp_report.txt. Analyze the eccentricity evolution and write a report to ~/GMAT_output/srp_eccentricity_analysis.txt containing the following labeled lines: max_eccentricity, min_eccentricity, ecc_circle_radius, ecc_oscillation_period_days, final_sma_km, and srp_enabled. Login: username=ga, password=password123
